text: Hello.
zh: 你好。

text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Beth.
zh: 我是贝丝。

text: It smells delicious, sizzling in oil and it's great for keeping vampires away.
zh: 它在油里滋滋作响,闻起来香气扑鼻,还能有效驱赶吸血鬼。

text: But don't eat it on a first date.
zh: 但是第一次约会时可别吃它。

text: I'm talking, of course, about garlic.
zh: 我说的当然是大蒜。

text: Neil, do you like garlic?
zh: 尼尔,你喜欢大蒜吗?

text: Oh yes, I love garlic.
zh: 哦,是的,我很喜欢大蒜。

text: I had a very garlicky meal last night.
zh: 我昨晚吃了一顿蒜味十足的饭。

text: Mmm, I know I can smell it from here.
zh: 嗯,我在这儿都能闻到味儿了。

text: is Jason Chan, an Australian chef who specialises in Asian cuisine,
zh: 是杰森·陈,一位专注亚洲菜系的澳大利亚厨师,

text: and Paul -Erik Jensen, a Danish chef living in France, talking about their love of garlic to BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: 还有保罗-埃里克·詹森,一位定居法国的丹麦厨师,他们在BBC世界服务节目《食物链》中谈到了自己对大蒜的热爱。

text: I love garlic because it's a versatile ingredient that can be used for cooking and offers various health benefits.
zh: 我喜欢大蒜,因为它是一种用途广泛的食材,可以用于烹饪,还有各种健康益处。

text: We use it a lot.
zh: 我们经常用它。

text: So, from stocks to soups and in vegetable dishes, meat dishes, there's very certain a clove of garlic in somewhere.
zh: 所以,从高汤到汤品,再到蔬菜菜肴、肉类菜肴,几乎肯定某个地方会用到一瓣大蒜。

text: Yeah, it's unimaginable not using garlic.
zh: 是啊,简直无法想象不用大蒜做饭。

text: Jason loves garlic because it's versatile, meaning it can be used in many different ways.
zh: 杰森喜欢大蒜是因为它用途多样,意思是可以用于很多不同的方式。

text: And Paul thinks cooking without garlic is unimaginable, meaning it's difficult to imagine because it would be so bad.
zh: 而保罗认为没有大蒜的烹饪简直难以想象,意思是因为效果会太差,让人难以想象。

text: love garlic or hate it.
zh: 喜欢大蒜或讨厌大蒜。

text: In this episode, we'll be finding out more about this versatile, strong -smelling food.
zh: 在这一集里,我们将进一步了解这种用途广泛、气味浓烈的食物。

text: And as usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases as well.
zh: 和往常一样,我们也会学习一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: And remember, you can find all the vocabulary from this episode on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记住,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到这一集的所有词汇。

text: Now, Beth, I have a question for you.
zh: 好了,贝丝,我有个问题要问你。

text: It's a little -known fact that of the 600 varieties of garlic, many grew only in the former Soviet Union and were unavailable in the West until the fall of communism in the 1990s.
zh: 有一个鲜为人知的事实:在600个大蒜品种中,许多只生长在前苏联地区,直到20世纪90年代共产主义垮台之前,在西方都买不到。

text: So, which of the following varieties of garlic grew in the former USSR?
zh: 那么,以下哪种大蒜品种曾在前苏联生长?

text: Is it a.
zh: 是a.

text: rocumbole, b.
zh: rocambole,还是b.

text: fire, or c.
zh: fire,还是c.

text: riesig?
zh: riesig?

text: Oh, I have no idea.
zh: 哦,我不知道。

text: I'm going to guess fire.
zh: 我猜是fire。

text: OK, we'll find out if you're right at the end of the programme.
zh: 好的,我们会在节目结尾揭晓你是否猜对。

text: Garlic is much more than just a cooking ingredient.
zh: 大蒜远不止是一种烹饪食材。

text: For centuries, garlic was an important food throughout ancient Egypt, Greece and Rome, as well as India and China.
zh: 几个世纪以来,大蒜在古埃及、希腊、罗马,以及印度和中国都是重要的食物。

text: It was the Roman Empire that brought garlic to European countries like France.
zh: 正是罗马帝国把大蒜带到了法国等欧洲国家。

text: And everywhere garlic grew, it was praised for its health -giving powers.
zh: 而无论大蒜生长在哪里,它都因其保健功效而备受赞誉。

text: Here, BBC presenter Rumela Dasgupta asks author and garlic expert Robin Cherry about garlic's medicinal uses on BBC World Service's The Food Chain.
zh: 接下来,BBC主持人鲁梅拉·达斯古普塔在BBC世界服务节目《食物链》中,采访了作家兼大蒜专家罗宾·切里,询问大蒜的药用价值。

text: And what kind of illnesses or conditions would they have treated, for instance?
zh: 举例来说,大蒜曾被用来治疗哪些疾病或病症呢?

text: Everything from cancer, scurvy, the plague, respiratory ailments, it was used as an aphrodisiac.
zh: 从癌症、坏血病、瘟疫,到呼吸系统疾病,应有尽有,它还被用作催情剂。

text: Garlic was used to treat respiratory ailments.
zh: 大蒜曾被用来治疗呼吸系统疾病。

text: An ailment is another word for an illness or minor health problem, so a respiratory ailment is an illness of the lungs.
zh: ailment是illness(疾病)或轻微健康问题的另一种说法,所以respiratory ailment指的是肺部疾病。

text: In ancient Greece, garlic was also used as an aphrodisiac, a food or drink believed to increase sexual desire.
zh: 在古希腊,大蒜也被用作aphrodisiac,也就是被认为能增强性欲的食物或饮品。

text: higher.
zh: 更高。

text: But interestingly, this was the same reason why some cultures avoided eating garlic altogether, including a religious group from India called Jains.
zh: 但有趣的是,正是出于同样的原因,一些文化完全避免食用大蒜,包括印度的一个宗教群体——耆那教徒。

text: Yes, Jainism is an ancient Indian religion, like Hinduism and Buddhism, based on the idea of uhimsa, or non -violence.
zh: 是的,耆那教是一种古老的印度宗教,和印度教、佛教一样,建立在ahimsa(非暴力)理念之上。

text: But unlike many Hindus and Buddhists, Jains' definition of of non -violence includes plants and vegetables.
zh: 但与许多印度教徒和佛教徒不同,耆那教徒对非暴力的定义还包括植物和蔬菜。

text: Janes don't eat garlic because uprooting the plant kills both it and the soil ecosystem it grows in.
zh: 耆那教徒不吃大蒜,因为拔起这种植物会同时杀死植株本身及其生长所依赖的土壤生态系统。

text: Here's Sonal Ved, a Mumbai -based cooking show host and author of many books on Indian cuisine,
zh: 这位是索纳尔·维德,一位常驻孟买的烹饪节目主持人,也是多部印度美食书籍的作者,

text: explaining another reason why Janes avoid garlic to BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: 她在BBC世界服务节目《食物链》中解释了耆那教徒回避大蒜的另一个原因。

text: According to Hinduism also, garlic is considered a food which is called the tamasik food.
zh: 根据印度教教义,大蒜也被视为一种叫做‘tamasik’(惰性)的食物。

text: food.
zh: 食物。

text: It is believed to stimulate desire, passion, aggression and that's why they abstain from eating those kinds of food because
zh: 人们相信它会刺激欲望、激情和攻击性,所以他们才不吃这类食物,因为

text: it is believed to impact your spiritual growth and also your mental clarity.
zh: 人们相信这会影响一个人的精神成长以及心智的清明。

text: Jane's abstain from eating garlic.
zh: 耆那教徒abstain(戒绝)食用大蒜。

text: If you abstain from doing something, you choose not to do it.
zh: 如果你abstain from做某事,就是选择不去做这件事。

text: For example, you might abstain from tobacco or alcohol because you know they're bad for your health.
zh: 例如,你可能会戒烟戒酒,因为你知道它们对健康有害。

text: Like the ancient Greeks, Janes believed that garlic stimulates sexual desire.
zh: 和古希腊人一样,耆那教徒也相信大蒜能stimulate(刺激)性欲。

text: The verb to stimulate means encourage it, or cause it to develop and grow.
zh: 动词stimulate的意思是鼓励某事发展,或使其产生和成长。

text: Modern medicine has confirmed some of these ancient beliefs about the health benefits of garlic, including reduced blood pressure and better gut health.
zh: 现代医学已经证实了一些关于大蒜健康益处的古老说法,包括降低血压和改善肠道健康。

text: OK, Neil, isn't it time you revealed the answer to your question?
zh: 好了,尼尔,是不是该公布你那个问题的答案了?

text: Yes, Beth, I asked you which variety of garlic grew in the former USSR.
zh: 是的,贝丝,我问你哪种大蒜品种曾生长在前苏联。

text: And I said fire.
zh: 我说的是fire。

text: Was I right?
zh: 我说对了吗?

text: You were absolutely correct.
zh: 你说得完全正确。

text: Fire, or Georgian fire, is a hot white garlic from the Republic of Georgia in the former Soviet Union.
zh: Fire,也叫Georgian fire(格鲁吉亚火蒜),是一种产自前苏联格鲁吉亚共和国的辛辣白皮大蒜。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with versatile – an adjective meaning to have many different purposes or uses.
zh: 好的,让我们回顾一下今天学到的词汇,首先是versatile——一个形容词,意思是有多种用途或功能。

text: The phrase it's unimaginable is used to say that something is difficult to imagine because it would be so good or so bad.
zh: 短语it's unimaginable用来形容某件事因为效果太好或太差而让人难以想象。

text: An ailment is an illness or health problem.
zh: ailment指的是一种疾病或健康问题。

text: An aphrodisiac is a food or drink believed to increase sexual desire.
zh: aphrodisiac是指被认为能增强性欲的食物或饮品。

text: If you abstain from something, you choose not to do it.
zh: 如果你abstain from something,就是选择不去做那件事。

text: And finally, to stimulate means to encourage something or cause it to grow.
zh: 最后,to stimulate的意思是鼓励某事,或促使其发展成长。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up.
zh: 我们的六分钟时间又到了。

text: If you've enjoyed this episode, then head over to our website bbclearningenglish .com where you'll find a quiz and worksheet to practice the vocabulary you've learnt.
zh: 如果你喜欢这一集节目,欢迎访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,那里有测验和练习题,帮助你巩固所学词汇。

text: See you again soon, but for now it's goodbye.
zh: 我们下次再见,现在先说再见。

text: Goodbye.
zh: 再见。
